notes: Node 2.30 adds an orphaned-process reaper. On Windows a node restart/kill leaves its ffmpeg children running (they don't die with the parent), so they pile up and lag the PC. Now: on startup the node kills every leftover Byte tool process (ffmpeg/dovi_tool/mkv*) from the previous instance, and a reaper runs every 60s to kill any Byte tool process not owned by an active job — active encodes are tracked by PID and never touched, and a just-spawned step is spared by a 45s grace. It only ever targets this node's own tool binaries, never unrelated ffmpeg. Updating the node (which restarts it) clears the current orphan pile-up immediately. Carries 2.29's temp-prep timeout guards.
